stem: A program needs to check whether a name appears in an unsorted list.
model_answer: A linear search starts at the first item in the list and compares it with the target. If it matches, the search ends and the position is returned. If it doesn't, the algorithm moves to the next item and repeats. If the end of the list is reached without a match, the target is not in the list. Linear search works on unsorted data but on average has to check half the list, making it slow for very large lists.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe how a linear search works.

mark_points.text: start at the first item in the list
compare each item in turn with the target value
accepted_alternatives: check items one by one against the value being searched for

mark_points.text: stop when a match is found or the end of the list is reached (not found)
accepted_alternatives: return the position if found / report not found at end of list
